Common question - 5 min read

Can Claude write my sales emails?

Yes. Claude writes excellent sales emails — meaningfully better than what most reps write themselves. But only when set up correctly. Without proper setup, the output reads like generic AI spam that recipients delete on sight. The difference is 90 minutes of setup work.

What makes the difference

Why most AI-written emails fail

Most AI-written sales emails sound generic because they are generated without context. A blank "write me a sales email" prompt produces template-shaped output that everyone recognizes as AI.

The fix is loading context: your ICP, your value prop, your voice, your past wins. Once loaded into a Claude Project, the output becomes specific, voice-matched, and indistinguishable from a good rep's writing.

See how to use AI to research prospects for the deeper workflow.

The setup that works

What you need to do once

Create a dedicated Claude Project called "Sales Outreach." Load: your operational ICP (15-25 fields), buyer personas, your value prop and positioning, 5-10 winning email examples (real ones), your voice guide.

Write a system prompt that tells Claude: who they are writing for, what voice to use, what to never do (AI tells like "I hope this finds you well", "circling back", "leveraging"), and to ask for clarifying info before drafting.

Test the Project on 3-5 real accounts before relying on it. Refine the system prompt based on what comes out.

After this 90-minute setup, every email you draft in the Project starts with full context. You go from blank page to ready-to-send in 5 minutes per email.

What the workflow looks like

The actual day-to-day

1. Paste account URL or LinkedIn profile into the Project.

2. Ask Claude to research the account (uses web search if you are on Pro/Team).

3. Ask Claude to draft 2-3 email variants based on the research.

4. Pick the variant that lands closest, edit for voice (usually 60 seconds).

5. Ship.

Time per email: 5-8 minutes. Quality: equal to or better than what a good rep would write in 30 minutes.
